items.title: Fifa reveal World Cup team of tournament with Bellingham and Man Utd star IN but winner left out for surprise journeyman
items.summary: JUDE BELLINGHAM is the only England hero to have been included in the World Cup's official team of the tournament. The Three Lions beat France to the bronze medal on Saturday to notch their best finish at the summer extravaganza since 1966. Bellingham, 23, made history of his own as he found the net

items.title: Liverpool confident of signing Barcola - Thursday's gossip
items.summary: Alex Scott favours a move to Arsenal, Bruno Fernandes is ready to commit his future to Manchester United, Liverpool are confident they will sign Bradley Barcola, plus more.

items.title: Arokodare forces Wolves training to be cancelled
items.summary: Wolves striker Tolu Arokodare forces the cancellation of a training session as he refuses to leave the pitch.

items.title: How Amazon boss Jeff Bezos and £200BILLION fortune could take Liverpool to next level and spearhead huge transfer spree
items.summary: LIVERPOOL’S links with Amazon tycoon Jeff Bezos could deliver the club to the next level. The Reds finished fifth last season – a mammoth 25 points behind Premier League winners Arsenal. So the news that Bezos has been approached to join forces with Amit Bhatia’s consortium and buy a minority stake

items.title: Finch: England's handling of Stokes and McCullum exits has been a circus
items.summary: Former Australia batter Aaron Finch likened England's recent coaching and captaincy upheaval to a 'circus' and says many Australians have been watching things unfold from afar 'giggling' at their old rivals.

items.title: McInnes glad of Rangers backing in appeal against ban
items.summary: Derek McInnes insists Rangers are right to contest the four-match touchline ban he received for describing as "disgusting" the decision to award Celtic a late penalty at Motherwell in May.
